items.title: Israel's bombardment of Gaza: The Impact on aid and Palestinians on the ground | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel is intensifying its military operations in Gaza. It says the new offensive aims to defeat Hamas and bring home hostages the militants are still holding. Palestinian officials say more than 150 people have been killed in the latest bombardments, which come during the third month of Israel's blockade of aid into the territory. 

Chapters:
0:00 Gaza officials: Israeli strikes kill more than 150 people
02:58 Rosalia Bollen, UNICEF

items.title: Concern grows over illegal nickel mining in Indonesia | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>There's growing concern in Indonesia over massive nickel mining operations polluting local bodies of water.

Around Kabaena Island in southeast Sulawesi, the sea has turned red and is now threatening the livelihoods of thousands. As DWâ€™s Ferdinand Himawan reports, the presence of the mines has also sparked tensions between the islandâ€™s Indigenous people and the Bajau, a nomadic seafaring community.

items.title: Gambling addiction among African youth reaches epidemic proportions | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>It has been called Africa's silent epidemic: gambling addiction. Across the continent, young people have become ideal targets for global betting companies, especially since 8 in 10 of them own mobile phones, often equipped with some form of payment. Or as one addict put it. "a casino in your pocket..  
The betting companies leverage young peoples' passion for football. And there's no passion greater than for the European leagues, where some clubs are even sponsored by betting companies, creating the impression there's little distinction between football and gambling.
Estimates on the size of Africa's gambling vary. But according to Astute Analytica, in 2023, the market generated a revenue of around 6 billion dollars. It's projected to almost double by 2032. 
As betting booms, critics are raising the alarm, saying its destroying lives. According to a geopoll study conducted last year among more than 3,000 people in 6 countries, 76% of people interviewed had gambled in Kenya, South Africa, Ghana, Uganda, Tanzania and Nigeria, with Kenya leading at 82%. This high engagement increases the risk of addiction, financial instability, and mental health issues. 
Now, the government in Kenya has banned  betting advertisements and promotions for 30 days, as it seeks to curb "a proliferation of gambling activities across the country." DWâ€™s East Africa Correspondent Felix Maringa met two men who say they simply can't break the habit.

00:00 Gambling addiction in Africa
05:03 DW speaks with Tunde Adebisi, researcher at Ulster University in Belfast, Northern Ireland

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Israel steps up strikes on Gaza as Arab League holds summit | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel's military says it has begun a major new offensive in Gaza, launching extensive airstrikes on the territory. Gaza's Hamas-run civil defense says more than 100 people were killed on Friday.
Israel's army released footage purporting to show strikes on Hamas military sites. Israel says the new offensive is aimed at freeing the hostages and dismantling Hamas, which carried out the October 7 terror attacks. 
The The United Nations high commissioner for human rights says Israel's actions are tantamount to ethnic cleansing and that Palestinians are being displaced and facing widespread starvation due to a monthslong blockade on humanitarian aid.
The war in Gaza is expected to dominate the annual summit of the Arab League, which kicks off today in Baghdad. 
The gathering in the Iraqi capital follows an emergency meeting of Arab leaders in Cairo in March. Back then, they agreed on a plan for the reconstruction of the Gaza Strip without displacing its roughly 2 million people. 

00:00 Israeli strikes in Gaza kill more than 100 people
03:00 DW's Fanny Facsar reports from Jerusalem
06:49 Arab League summit in Bahdad focuses on Gaza
07:13 Journalist Karim El-Gawhary reports from Cairo

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Fighting continues in Ukraine after first talks in three years stall | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Ukrainian military says at least eight people have been killed by a Russian drone attack on a civilian bus in Ukraine's Sumy region. The strike came just hours after the first direct peace talks between Ukraine and Russia since 2022 ended with no breakthrough. Negotiators agreed on a prisoner swap - but failed to produce a ceasefire or a meeting between their two leaders. Ukraine says Russia demanded control of more territory as a pre-condition for a truce.
DW's Rebecca Ritters Reports from Kyiv, and Sergei Santanovskii reports from Riga.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What could compel Russia to start serious peace talks? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russia and Ukraine held peace talks in Istanbul today. It was the first direct meeting in more than three years. The talks ended after less than two hours with no deal. 
Ukraine says Russia made new demands, including a military pullback from key regions - conditions which Kyiv says are unacceptable. But both sides did agree to a major prisoner swap. One thousand detainees each. And the two sides agreed in principle to meet again. 

00:00 Ukraine-Russia peace talks come to a close
04:32 Andrei Kozyrev, Former Russian Foreign Minister
